title: Reader API Caching, ETags, Preconditions, and Invalidation · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs reader api caching, etags, preconditions, and invalidation from revision, tenant, source URL, request validators, response bytes, parsed identity, transaction, lease, state transition, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for privacy-safe conditional reader API after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Classify public, tenant-private, user-private, sensitive, non-storeable, and invalidation-sensitive representations before fields are set.
Derive validators from authorized representation version, query, locale, content negotiation, and principal scope and choose weak or strong semantics.
Evaluate If-None-Match precedence and If-Modified-Since correctly after authentication and representation selection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Feed Go Invalidation Proof
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go team handles case 9444 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile private cache classification, ETags, conditional reads, write preconditions, and invalidation; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test subscribe, ingest, edit, delete, read-state, authorization, locale, and deployment changes against cache keys and validators.

Inspect the Feed Go Invalidation Proof trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Feed Go Invalidation Proof.

At Feed aggregation and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Feed Go Cache Classification, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go team handles case 4066 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile private cache classification, ETags, conditional reads, write preconditions, and invalidation; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify public, tenant-private, user-private, sensitive, non-storeable, and invalidation-sensitive representations before fields are set.

For Feed aggregation and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Feed Go Cache Classification, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: GET responses are safe for shared caches by default.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Invalidation Proof
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Feed Go Invalidation Proof: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go team handles case 5685 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile private cache classification, ETags, conditional reads, write preconditions, and invalidation; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test subscribe, ingest, edit, delete, read-state, authorization, locale, and deployment changes against cache keys and validators.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Invalidation Proof.
steps.why: Retrieving Feed Go Invalidation Proof through the concrete privacy-safe conditional reader API case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Invalidation Proof, keep Feed Go Invalidation Proof connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test subscribe, ingest, edit, delete, read-state, authorization, locale, and deployment changes against cache keys and validators.
